articles.title: The world's fastest spider tops 3.5 metres per second
articles.summary: The most comprehensive database ever compiled of how fast arachnids can run has shown how leg anatomy and evolutionary history influence spiders’ running speed

articles.title: Scientists discover a completely different way to fight viruses

articles.title: What is 'SpudCell'? Arguably the greatest bioengineering feat yet
articles.summary: A prototype cell partly capable of replicating itself has been created using 36 existing bacterial genes, but it's not really a living organism – yet

articles.title: New calculator reveals whether you should really worry about statin side effects

articles.title: Rescue mission launches to save NASA telescope that's falling back to Earth
articles.summary: A three-armed spacecraft rocketed into orbit Friday to rescue a NASA telescope that's in danger of crashing back to Earth.

articles.title: Sea turtles diving through the eye of the storm help develop better cyclone forecasts
articles.summary: Every summer, communities across northern Australia brace for the tropical cyclone season. Tropical cyclones draw their power from the warm seas, extracting heat and moisture from ocean water.

articles.title: Projected 'super typhoon' heads for US Pacific islands
articles.summary: Residents of Guam and the Northern Marianas boarded up windows and stocked up Friday as a monster weather system forecast to become a "super typhoon" approached the U.S. Pacific territories.

articles.title: Wildfires rage in southern France, 3,000 people evacuated
